heading: GENERAL PROVISIONS
label: 1 GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: This title was enacted by ,act July 30, 1947, ch. 388, § 161 Stat. 633

Positive Law; CitationThis title has been made positive law by section 1 of , , which provided in part that:act July 30, 1947, ch. 38861 Stat. 633“Title 1 of the United States Code entitled ‘General Provisions’, is codified and enacted into positive law and may be cited as ‘1 U. S. C., § ——.’ ”

Repeals, , provided that the sections or parts thereof of the Statutes at Large or the Revised Statutes covering provisions codified in this Act are repealed insofar as the provisions appeared in former Title 1, and provided that any rights or liabilities now existing under the repealed sections or parts thereof shall not be affected by the repeal.Act July 30, 1947, ch. 388, § 261 Stat. 640

Writs of Error, , provided that:Act June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 2362 Stat. 990“All Acts of Congress referring to writs of error shall be construed as amended to the extent necessary to substitute appeal for writ of error.”
